preFix.title: Willow potato basket
preFix.subtitle: A sturdy oval storage basket in brown willow
preFix.excerpt: A wide oval basket in brown willow for storing potatoes and root vegetables. An oval slath base, straight randed sides, and a simple three-rod border. A good first willow project: simpler than the trug, with a flat base and no handle to fit.
preFix.sourceNotes: Cassell's Cyclopaedia of Mechanics vol. III for oval-base basket construction. Beeton's Book of Household Management (1861) for potato and root-vegetable storage baskets in the domestic larder.

content.text: A potato basket is a wide, low oval basket about 50 cm long and 35 cm wide. The flat base and straight sides make it straightforward to weave: there is no taper to manage and no handle to fit. Brown willow is the traditional material, giving a sturdy basket that handles the weight of root vegetables.
Soaking

Soak all rods for twelve to twenty-four hours before working. Test readiness by bending a rod through a right angle: if it holds without cracking, it is ready; if it snaps, return it to the trough for four more hours. Keep rods submerged with a weight.
The oval base
Lay five long base sticks in parallel along the long axis of the planned oval. Thread three short slath sticks through holes pierced in the long sticks to form the
slath
. Pair-weave two thin rods around the slath for two circuits to lock the base open and separate the sticks. Then rand with single rods until the base reaches 50 cm long and 35 cm wide. Close with a
waling
row at the outer edge of the base.
Staking up

Insert side stakes alongside each base spoke at the waling line. Use a bodkin to open the weave and push each stake butt-end in alongside its base spoke. Bend each stake upright at the waling border and tie the tops together temporarily. Begin side weaving with three rows of waling to lock the stakes vertical.
The sides
Weave the sides with
randing

: one rod over one stake, under one stake, around the basket. Keep the stakes vertical throughout. Pack each row firmly down with the rapping iron before adding the next. Continue randing to 20 cm height. Finish with three rows of waling to stiffen the rim area before the border.
The border

Work a three-rod border to close the top. Take each stake, bend it down behind the next stake to the right, and weave a three-step sequence before trimming the end flush. Trim all weaving ends with secateurs. Let the basket dry in a ventilated space for two weeks before loading with produce.
Variations
Larger root cellar basket: extend the base to 65 cm. Add two rope handles at the short ends for carrying when full.
Shopping basket: reduce the base to 35 cm x 25 cm. Add a single central handle bow using a thick soaked rod.

rows.fix: Check the spacing after the first two rows of pair-weaving and correct before continuing. A card template cut to the target oval dimensions helps.
rows.cause: Slath sticks not held at the correct spacing during the first weaving rows
rows.symptom: Base is round rather than oval

rows.fix: Add one extra stake per curve section. On oval baskets, stakes at the curved ends need to be closer together than on the straight sides.
rows.cause: Stake spacing too wide on the tight curves
rows.symptom: Randing rows are loose on the curved ends of the oval

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
